actions.thought: Case 1 needs orange, brown, green - Mazok Shop has all three. As Sheriff I take 2 tokens regardless of collision.
Mazok Shop has green and brown which is exactly what I need for Thin Wall.
Eilat Reef has 3 blue tokens - alone rule gives me 2, covering entire blue requirement for Case 10.
actions.confusion: Does Sheriff status always override Priority for token allocation in 2-player collisions?

actions.thought: Everyone's going to pile on Eilat Reef. Dizengoff — only 2 tokens, nobody wants it, but with priority 7 I take both uncontested. Primes me toward public Dracula Cape case.
actions.confusion: Is draining a 2-token location intended? Alone takes 2, Dizengoff only HAS 2.

collisions.resolution_rule: Sheriff bonus: Danny takes 2, Michal takes 1

outcomes: Collision at Mazok: Danny(sheriff) 2, Michal 1. Sheriff moved to Michal (fewest tokens).

actions.thought: Dizengoff has orange+brown - if alone I take both and immediately complete Case 1.
Mazok has two greens and I need them for Thin Wall. As sheriff, even if Danny follows I take both!
Need exactly 1 green to complete Case 10. Mazok has 2 green.

ability_triggered.thought: Collision with sheriff Michal = empty-handed. Ability draws 2 from bag.

actions.thought: Pivoting to public Case 4 Dracula Cape. Just need 1 green. Subaru has 2 greens.

collisions.resolution_rule: Sheriff bonus: Michal takes 2 green, Yossi gets 0 — ability triggers

outcomes: 3 completions in one round! Danny, Yossi (via ability), Ronit (public case). Michal got 2 green but no completion.

referee_flags.content: Yermi's 'You Know' ability: collision → empty-handed → draw 2 from bag → complete case in same round. Ability + completion in one phase is very powerful.
Public Case 4 completed by Ronit who stockpiled darkred at Dizengoff. Public cases create viable alternative strategies.

actions.thought: Dizengoff has orange+darkred - exactly what I need to complete Case 19.
Subaru has brown — I need one brown for Thin Wall. Going alone means completing my case!
Case 7 needs green+darkred+blue. Eilat has blue+darkred — alone I get 2 of 3 needed.
Nadav has blue+brown — both needed for Case 5 Oracle Mystery. One trip closes it.

outcomes: No collisions — all 4 went to different locations. 3 more completions. Danny and Ronit at 2/3.

referee_flags.content: 4 rounds, 0 collisions — players successfully spread across 8 locations. With 4 players and 8 locations, collision probability per round is low (~35%).

actions.thought: Subaru has blue+brown — two of three for Case 11. Quieter than Rosa.
Barka has blue and darkred — two of my three tokens for Case 14 Photographer in one trip!
Need 1 green. Dizengoff has green+orange. Quieter than Mazok.
Need green+orange for Case 17. Dizengoff has both.
actions.collision_note: Ronit P7 picks first, takes green. Yossi P3 gets orange.

collisions.resolution_rule: No sheriff, priority resolves: Ronit P7 first, Yossi P3 second

outcomes: Collision at Dizengoff: Ronit takes green (needed), Yossi gets orange (not needed). Michal completes Case 14 — now 3 players at 2/3! Sheriff to Yossi.

referee_flags.content: Green token scarcity: only 2 green on board across all 8 locations. Both at Mazok+Dizengoff. Creates severe bottleneck for multiple players.
Priority advantage in non-sheriff collisions: Ronit P7 denied Yossi P3 the only green he needed. Priority as tiebreaker is decisive at 2-token locations.

actions.thought: Need 1 orange to win. Mazok has orange and is less obvious than Rosa. Even in collision I should get something.

ability_triggered.thought: 3-way collision at Mazok = empty-handed. Milano Express grabs 2 orange from uncontested Rosa!

actions.thought: Nadav has darkred AND orange — two of my three tokens for Singapore case.
Only 1 green on board. As Sheriff I take both tokens regardless of collision. Also blocks Danny from winning.
Mazok is the only green on the board. Priority P7 means I take first after sheriff.
actions.confusion: Simultaneous completion tiebreaker rule needed for 3-case wins in same round.

collisions.resolution_rule: 3+ players: Sheriff(Yossi) takes 1 green, next priority(Ronit P7) takes 1 orange, Danny(P2) gets nothing — Milano Express triggers

outcomes: CLIMACTIC FINISH: 3-way collision at Mazok. Danny empty-handed, Milano Express grabs orange from Rosa, completes Case 11 for the WIN (3/3)! Yossi also completes Case 7 (2/3).

referee_flags.content: Milano Express (Mai, P2) is the strongest ability in the game. Guarantees 2 tokens from any uncontested location regardless of current collision. Combined with lowest priority (most likely to trigger), it's essentially a free turn.

3-player collision at 2-token location: sheriff gets 1, P7 gets 1, P2 gets 0. Lowest priority player is systematically disadvantaged in multi-way collisions. Milano Express compensates, but without it Danny would have been blocked.

referee_flags.resolution: Same as section 1.5: fewest excess tokens, then lower priority

player_confusions.content: Maintain 2 private cases vs start with 1 in 4p

key_findings: F-005: 'You Know' ability + completion in same round — very powerful combo
F-006: Public cases enable alternative strategies (darkred stockpiling)
F-007: Green token scarcity in later rounds — replenishment RNG can create severe bottlenecks
F-008: Priority advantage decisive at 2-token locations in non-sheriff collisions
F-009: Milano Express is arguably the strongest ability — guaranteed 2 tokens from any location
F-010: 3-player collision at 2-token location: lowest priority gets nothing
F-011: Dead locations: Messy Closet and Rosa Salon unvisited in 5 rounds
F-012: 4p setup ambiguity: maintain 2 private cases vs start with 1
